originalTitle: Australia news live: mourners arrive for Derryn Hinch state funeral; Mark Dreyfus tells antisemitism inquiry of ‘deeply wounding’ attack
content: More mourners arrive at Derryn Hinch’s state funeral 3AW presenter Denis Walter, the master of ceremonies for broadcaster Derryn Hinch’s state funeral, has arrived at the Timber Yard in Melbourne, as has 10 News journalist and presenter Jennifer Keyte. South Australian electorate renamed after Lowitja O’Donoghue Lowitja O’Donoghue, a trailblazing Aboriginal nurse turned Indigenous rights advocate, has been honoured with the renaming of a federal electorate, AAP reports. The vast division of Grey, which covers the majority of South Australia, is being renamed as O’Donoghue, the Australian Electoral Commission announced on Monday. O’Donoghue, a member of the Stolen Generations, was the first Aboriginal woman to be trained as a nurse and served as the founding chairperson of both the National Aboriginal Conference and the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Commission. Her advocacy was pivotal to Australia’s native title legislation. O’Donoghue was a Yankunytjatjara woman, with the electorate of Grey taking in most of the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara lands. The AEC determined the name change despite 235 of the 310 community submissions arguing to retain Grey, named for Sir George Grey. The AEC found the arguments for change were “well detailed, informative, convincing and ultimately persuasive. This contrasted with most of the submissions objecting to the renaming of the electorate.” The commission noted Sir George spent “a comparatively small part of his life” in Australia, with longer service to the British empire in South Africa and New Zealand, and the AEC decided “the time had come to recognise the outstanding contribution to Australia and its people made by a distinguished Australian”. The decision comes after an AEC proposal last week to rename the southern Tasmanian seat of Franklin to Tongerlongeter, after an Indigenous warrior who fought colonisation, then took on a leadership role for Aboriginal Tasmanians exiled to Flinders Island. The AEC has opened consultations on the Franklin switch, which comes after a radical re-drawing of the electorate’s boundaries. Former colleagues, friends and associates of the late Derryn Hinch have begun arriving at the state funeral service for the former journalist and senator. The media personality died earlier this month after a long battle with poor health. Greg Evans, Ray Martin and Denis Walter have been spotted arriving at the Timber Yard in Melbourne for the midday service. Dreyfus says he feels ‘freer as an MP’ to support Jewish community after leaving cabinet Mark Dreyfus has described feeling “freer as an MP” to represent the Jewish community after he was replaced as attorney general by Michelle Rowland. He said one of the reasons he had given evidence to the commission was the “unique position” his role as a Jewish member of the federal cabinet had put him in after the start of the conflict in Gaza. He said: I wasn’t in a position to respond fully, or perhaps at all, to the attacks and demands that have been made of me by some Jewish Australians, simply because I was a cabinet minister and the constraints of the office meant that I was not in a position to respond or comment. Dreyfus said since leaving the cabinet in a reshuffle after the 2025 federal election, “I’ve been much freer as an MP”. I was able to respond after the Bondi attack … able to respond publicly, able to appear in the media, able to give speeches, able to make representations to cabinet ministers about what the government might be able to do, and it’s certainly, to some extent, been liberating compared to what I experienced, the constraints I experienced as a cabinet minister. Dreyfus also described an incident in May 2024 in which his office was covered with red paint and “Who does AG Mark Dreyfus serve?” written on the front pavement as a “deeply wounding attack”. He said: No one ever should make that suggestion about a member of the Australian parliament, let alone someone who had been sworn to administer a portfolio as the attorney general of the commonwealth, but it invokes all of those old antisemitic tropes about dual loyalties and treachery to Australia, all of which are false. It’s a deeply wounding attack … and probably intended to be. Andrew Charlton says he was part of class action after book scraped by AI company More on AI from Andrew Charlton. He told Sky earlier this morning that he was part of a class action in the United States after a book he wrote was scraped by a major AI company. By way of explaining the jurisdictional constraints on copyright law in relation to AI companies already scraping the work of Australian creators, Charlton gave the example of his own work: You know, I wrote a book nearly 10 years ago. That book was scraped by an AI giant from a pirated website. My book was the subject of a piece of litigation, and that litigation was found in favour of me and the other authors. But that occurred in the United States and that litigation happened in the United States. And the award is occurring in the United States … It was a class action. My book was part of that class action. He continued: The prime minister has been very clear that [AI] training that occurs in Australia will support Australian artists. That is what we have control over. The federal MP for Isaacs and former attorney general, Mark Dreyfus, has told the royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion about the “breach of that sense of safety” experienced by Jewish Australians since 7 October. The fifth block of hearings, starting in Melbourne this week, is examining security arrangements for the Jewish community, including the role of Jewish community security groups (CSGs) and their coordination with law enforcement agencies. At the start of this morning’s hearing, counsel assisting Richard Lancaster SC said evidence to the commission would show “a substantial and ongoing threat to the safety and security of the Jewish community in Australia”. chineseTitle: 澳大利亚新闻直播：哀悼者抵达参加德林·辛奇的国葬；马克·德雷福斯告诉反犹太主义调查“严重伤害”袭击
chineseContent: 更多哀悼者抵达 Derryn Hinch 的国葬 广播公司 Derryn Hinch 国葬的司仪 3AW 主持人 Denis Walter 已抵达墨尔本 Timber Yard，10 News 记者兼主持人 Jennifer Keyte 也抵达墨尔本。据澳联社报道，南澳大利亚州选区以洛维贾·奥多诺霍 (Lowitja O’Donoghue) 的名字重新命名。洛维贾·奥多诺霍 (Lowitja O’Donoghue) 是一位开拓性的原住民护士，后来成为原住民权利倡导者，他很荣幸能够为联邦选区重新命名。澳大利亚选举委员会周一宣布，覆盖南澳大利亚大部分地区的格雷区将更名为奥多诺霍。奥多诺霍是“被偷走的一代”的成员，她是第一位接受护士培训的原住民女性，并担任全国原住民会议以及原住民和托雷斯海峡岛民委员会的创始主席。她的倡导对于澳大利亚土著产权立法至关重要。奥多诺霍是一名 Yankunytjatjara 女性，格雷选民占领了 Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara 的大部分土地。尽管 310 份社区意见书中有 235 份主张保留以乔治·格雷爵士 (Sir George Grey) 命名的格雷 (Grey)，但 AEC 仍决定更改名称。澳大利亚选举委员会发现，变革的论据“非常详细、信息丰富、令人信服且最终具有说服力。这与大多数反对重新命名选民的意见书形成鲜明对比。”该委员会指出，乔治爵士在澳大利亚度过了“相对较小的一生”，在南非和新西兰为大英帝国服务的时间更长，AEC 决定“是时候表彰一位杰出的澳大利亚人对澳大利亚及其人民做出的杰出贡献了”。这一决定是在 AEC 上周提议重新命名南部地区之后作出的。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
